channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Global geopolitical news headlines curated by Frontion

item.title: Moldova's former powerful oligarch Plahotniuc sentenced to 19 years in prison
item.description: Vladimir Plahotniuc, Moldova's wealthiest businessman who de facto controlled the country's government in the 2010s, was sentenced in absentia. His fall is seen as part of Moldova's alignment with European democratic values.

item.title: Germany unveils strategy to become Europe's strongest military by 2039
item.description: The Bundeswehr will move away from rigid hardware quotas toward a flexible, effects-based planning model. The plan comes amid concerns about Russian military buildup.

item.title: Russia could be ready for NATO conflict a year after Ukraine, Dutch intelligence warns
item.description: Moscow's goal would not be to defeat NATO militarily but to divide it through limited territorial gains, according to a new Dutch intelligence report.

item.title: Canada PM hits back after Trump team demands 'entry fee' before trade talks
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney rejected US demands for concessions before trade negotiations could begin, pushing back against the Trump administration's approach.

item.title: Baltic nations brace for Iran war impact on US weapons shipments
item.description: Delays in ammunition deliveries could reopen a thorny question for Baltic HIMARS users: Would the US permit non-Lockheed rockets to be loaded?

item.title: Iran Seizes Two Cargo Ships in Strait of Hormuz After Firing on Three Vessels
item.description: Iran fired on three ships in the Strait of Hormuz and seized two of them, escalating maritime tensions despite Trump extending the US-Iran ceasefire. The MSC Francesca and Epaminondas were taken to Iranian ports.

item.title: EU Approves €90 Billion Ukraine Loan as Druzhba Pipeline Restarts
item.description: The EU gave preliminary approval to the massive loan for Ukraine after Hungary dropped its veto following the restart of Russian oil flows through the Druzhba pipeline. The package includes the 20th round of Russia sanctions.

item.title: Nigeria Charges Six With Treason in Alleged Coup Plot Against President Tinubu
item.description: Six people including a retired major general and a police inspector pleaded not guilty to plotting to overthrow President Bola Tinubu. The case follows the cancelled Independence Day parade in October 2025.

item.title: Pope Leo Condemns Prison Conditions in Equatorial Guinea, Calls for Justice
item.description: Speaking to 100,000 people in the oil-rich nation, Pope Leo criticized the treatment of prisoners and urged that the country's resources benefit all citizens, not just the elite. He is visiting a notorious prison in Bata.

item.title: US Justice Department Withdraws Subpoenas in John Brennan Investigation
item.description: The DOJ reversed course on subpoenas in the investigation of former CIA Director John Brennan, now requesting voluntary interviews. A Trump loyalist, Joe diGenova, was installed to lead the inquiry.

item.title: NATO Launches First Response to Suspected Deep-Sea Cable Sabotage
item.description: NATO conducted its first-ever operation in response to suspected sabotage of undersea communications cables, deploying vessels to the North Sea after intelligence suggested deliberate targeting of critical infrastructure.

item.title: Rwanda-Backed Rebels Enter Congo Safe Haven City, Aid Hub Threatened
item.description: Rwanda-backed M23 rebels have entered Goma, a key humanitarian hub in eastern Congo, raising fears for civilians and aid operations in the conflict zone.

item.title: Italy Supports Saudi Arabia Joining GCAP Fighter Jet Program, PM Meloni Says
item.description: Italy backs Saudi Arabia's participation in the Global Combat Air Programme, the multinational sixth-generation fighter jet project also involving the UK and Japan.

item.title: Climate Change Threat to Democracy Extends Beyond Elections, Report Warns
item.description: Extreme weather events, heatwaves, floods, and wildfires are increasingly threatening democratic institutions and electoral processes worldwide, according to a new Guardian global development report.

item.title: Pope Leo Visit Snubbed by African Leaders Amid Diplomatic Tensions
item.description: Pope Leo's visit to Africa has been marked by diplomatic tensions, with some leaders appearing to avoid formal meetings, highlighting complex geopolitical dynamics.

item.title: Iran Ramps Up Attacks on Ships in Strait of Hormuz After Trump Ceasefire Extension
item.description: Despite the ceasefire extension, Iran has intensified attacks on commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, raising concerns about the sustainability of the pause in hostilities.

item.title: F-15EX Buy Doubled by USAF in Major Fighter Fleet Shift
item.description: The US Air Force has doubled its F-15EX Eagle II purchase, a significant shift in fighter procurement strategy that makes perfect sense given current threats.

item.title: Iran Deployed Mines in Hormuz Could Take Up to Six Months to Clear
item.description: Iranian mines deployed in the Strait of Hormuz during the conflict could require months of clearance operations, potentially disrupting global shipping for an extended period.

item.title: Israel's Dependence on Chinese Drones Exposes IDF's Soft Underbelly
item.description: Israel's military reliance on Chinese-made drone components presents a significant vulnerability, exposing supply chain risks in its defense industry.

item.title: Iran War Has Used Up Half of US Patriot and THAAD Missile Stockpiles
item.description: The ongoing Iran conflict has depleted approximately half of US Patriot and THAAD missile inventories, raising concerns about American air defense readiness.

item.title: China Building Artificial Islands in South China Sea for Military Expansion
item.description: New satellite imagery reveals China's continued construction of artificial islands in the South China Sea, part of a broader military expansion strategy in disputed waters.

item.title: Iran Receives Billions in Cash From US in Iraq Shipment
item.description: The US has authorized a shipment of billions of dollars in cash to Iran through Iraq, part of ongoing negotiations and ceasefire arrangements between Washington and Tehran.

item.title: Russia Rebuilds Occupied Territories With Forced Population Transfers
item.description: Moscow is methodically rebuilding occupied Ukrainian territories while forcing population transfers, creating new demographic realities in areas like Mariupol.

item.title: Americans Question CIA Role After Mexico Plane Crash Kills Agents
item.description: A plane crash in Mexico that killed American agents has raised questions about CIA operations south of the border and the scope of US intelligence activities.

item.title: Millions in India Stripped of Vote Before Critical State Election
item.description: Electoral rolls have been purged in key Indian states ahead of critical elections, with millions of voters removed from rolls in what critics call political manipulation.
